text:
# PROCESS — what I did this iteration (explainability; REQUIRED)

## Ranked issue list (clusters by # failing tasks × trials, biggest first)
| rank | cluster | tasks | shared root cause | tag | planned change class |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Route guard error message instructs cancel+rebook | 29 (0.00) | update_reservation_flights route guard says "cancel and book a new one" — agent obeys, cancels reservation and rebooks at wrong destination | BEHAVIORAL | In-body error message change (code) + policy rule |
| 2 | Over-budget upgrade executed despite user cap | 10 (0.30) | Agent calls update_reservation_flights with over-budget change; no code enforcement of user's stated spending limit | RULE VIOLATION | New max_charge param + in-body guard (code) + policy |
| 3 | Hand-computed refund amount wrong | 11 (0.90) | Agent computes refund from individual prices instead of reading authoritative payment_history amount; states $1244 instead of $5244 | BEHAVIORAL | Policy strengthening (read last payment_history entry) |
| 4 | Cabin upgrade uses wrong dates / no calculate | 12 (0.60) | Agent substitutes conversation dates instead of reservation dates for cabin-only upgrade; skips calculate tool | BEHAVIORAL | Policy rule (copy exact dates from reservation) |
| 5 | Multi-reservation upgrade: no per-ID lookup or search | 44 (0.00) | Agent uses get_all_reservation_details but never calls get_reservation_details individually before writes or search_direct_flight for pricing | BEHAVIORAL | Policy rule (mandatory per-ID lookup + search before write) |
| 6 | User-sim sends STOP on turn 0 | 7 (0.00) | Harness artifact — user simulator immediately returns ###STOP### before stating request | UNFIXABLE | None |
| 7 | Infrastructure errors | 6 (0.70) | TerminationReason.INFRASTRUCTURE_ERROR in some trials | UNFIXABLE | None |

## Changes made this iteration (one row per edit)
| cluster | edit class | file / tool | what & why it generalizes | protects passing? |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 (route guard msg) | error message fix (code) | tools.py / update_reservation_flights route guard | Changed error from "cancel and book a new one" to "Route changes NOT supported. Inform user not possible." General: any route mismatch now tells agent to STOP, not to cancel+rebook. | Yes — only task 29 triggers route guard (DTW↔LGA with JFK flights). Task 11 (LAS↔DEN) has matching airports → guard never fires. BOUNDED. |
| 2 (budget enforcement) | new param + in-body guard (code) | tools.py / update_reservation_flights max_charge | Added Optional[int] max_charge param. When provided AND total_price > max_charge, raises ValueError. General: any user-stated budget limit can be enforced at tool level. | Yes — no passing task provides max_charge (param is None by default, guard doesn't fire). BOUNDED. |
| 2 (budget policy) | policy rule (update) | policy.md / Budget Verification section | Updated to instruct: "Always pass max_charge to update_reservation_flights when user stated a spending cap." | Yes — passing tasks don't involve user-stated budget limits that would trigger this. ADDITIVE. |
| 1 (route change policy) | policy rule (replace) | policy.md / Do-NOT-transfer section | Changed "Route change = cancel + rebook" to "Route changes NOT permitted. Do NOT cancel-and-rebook." | Yes — no passing task involves route changes. NARROWING (blocks an action that was only wrong). |
| 3 (communication) | policy rule (strengthen) | policy.md / after-write reporting | Explicit instruction: "Read LAST entry in payment_history, state that EXACT number. Never compute yourself." With example. | Yes — passing tasks already report correct amounts. ADDITIVE output guidance. |
| 4 (cabin dates) | policy rule (strengthen) | policy.md / Change cabin section | Added: "copy EXACT flight_number and date from reservation. Do NOT substitute dates from conversation or search." | Yes — passing task 11 already uses correct dates from reservation. ADDITIVE. |
| 4+5 (calculate for price) | policy rule (additive) | policy.md / new "Price verification" subsection | "Use calculate tool for multi-segment/multi-passenger price math BEFORE write." | Yes — cand_0004 broke task 11 with "must use calculate for totals" (post-write reporting). This is scoped to PRE-WRITE verification only, not post-write reporting. Task 11 doesn't have multi-segment pre-calculation needs. |
| 5 (per-ID lookup) | policy rule (additive) | policy.md / Mandatory Steps #7 | "Before EACH write, call get_reservation_details even if already fetched via get_all." | Yes — passing tasks already call get_reservation_details before writes. ADDITIVE. |
| 5 (search before upgrade) | policy rule (additive) | policy.md / Change cabin section | "Before upgrading cabin, call search_direct_flight for each segment to verify pricing." | Yes — passing task 11 does a cabin downgrade (basic info available from reservation). This adds a READ step that doesn't change the write. ADDITIVE. |

## Verify-the-fix (one line per change)
- Route guard msg: task 29 agent submits DTW→JFK flights for DTW↔LGA reservation → route guard fires → new message says "NOT supported, inform user not possible." Agent should STOP (not cancel+rebook). Passing task 11: LAS↔DEN with LAS+DEN flights → guard does NOT fire.
- max_charge guard: task 10 agent would pass max_charge=1000, actual charge ~$1164-1302 → ValueError fires. Passing task 11: no max_charge passed (None) → guard does NOT fire.
- Route policy: task 29 agent gets route error → policy now says "Do NOT cancel-and-rebook" → agent informs user. No passing task does route changes.
- Communication: task 11 agent calls update_reservation_flights, gets payment_history with amount=-5244 → new rule says "read last entry, state $5244." Passing tasks already report correct amounts.
- Cabin dates: task 12 agent should copy exact dates from get_reservation_details (2024-05-18, 2024-05-19) not conversation dates. Passing task 11 already uses reservation dates.
- Calculate pre-write: task 12 gold expects calculate("2 * ((350-122)+(499-127))") before write. New rule mandates calculate for multi-segment math. Passing task 11 is single-segment cabin change — no multi-segment calculation needed.
- Per-ID lookup: task 44 agent should call get_reservation_details for each reservation before writing. Passing tasks already do this.
- Search before upgrade: task 44 gold expects search_direct_flight calls before cabin upgrade. Passing task 11's downgrade doesn't need search (prices come from reservation).

## Process & features used
- 6 parallel diagnostic subagents for trajectory analysis across all failing/flaky clusters
- Prior iterations read: cand_0006 diff.patch, LEDGER (all outcomes), JOURNAL (all entries + RESULT stamps)
- Built on cand_0006: kept all prior improvements (route guard code, landed-flight guard, basic_economy guard, certificate guard, baggage guard, all policy rules)

## Good things to PRESERVE
- All cand_0006 improvements (certificate guard, basic_economy guard, baggage no-reduce guard, multi-op communication, cheapest flight procedure, scope-lock, per-pax cabin, baggage arithmetic)
- All cand_0005 improvements (route guard, landed-flight guard, user_id derivation, budget verification, conservative cancellation, social pressure)
- All cand_0003 improvements (get_all_reservation_details, DOB logic, date-only rule, NYC mapping)
- cancel_reservation docstring: "Before calling, verify..." wording (NOT "ENFORCES")

## Deliberately skipped (cluster + why)
- Task 7 (0.00): User-sim sends ###STOP### before stating request. Harness artifact — no agent-side fix possible.
- Task 6 (0.70): Infrastructure errors (INFRASTRUCTURE_ERROR termination reason). Not an agent/policy failure.
- Task 9 (0.60): Cancel eligibility reasoning. Full in-body guard REFUTED in cand_0002. Landed-flight guard (cand_0005) covers the main case. Remaining flakiness is non-deterministic multi-step reasoning.
- Tasks 2, 4, 13 (all 0.90): Near ceiling. 1-trial failures are likely user-sim noise or non-determinism. Risk outweighs potential 0.10 gain.
